skill.name: motif-logo-generator
skill.description: Generate publication-quality sequence logos for DNA or protein motifs to visualize conserved positions in aligned sequences.
skill.summary: Generate sequence logos from DNA or protein sequence alignments to visualize conserved positions.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 52 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The weak cryptographic algorithm hits are signature collisions in non-cryptographic code (argparse setup, YAML frontmatter). All 36 external_commands findings reference shell commands within markdown documentation code blocks, not automated execution. The path traversal and hidden file access findings are from a security checklist and an installation path example. The skill performs legitimate bioinformatics operations: reading aligned sequences, calculating information content, and writing output logo files.

content.user_title: Generate Sequence Motif Logos from DNA or Protein Alignments
content.value_statement: Create meaningful visualizations of conserved sequence positions from aligned DNA or protein sequences. This skill calculates information content at each position and renders sequence logos in ASCII or WebLogo format for publication and analysis.
actual_capabilities: Generate ASCII sequence logos showing residue conservation at each position in aligned sequences
Calculate information content in bits per position using frequency-based methods
Output WebLogo command instructions for publication-quality PNG logo generation
Process DNA or protein sequences from input files or built-in demo data
Save results to user-specified output file paths
limitations: Only processes pre-aligned sequences of equal length; does not perform sequence alignment
ASCII logo uses block characters for frequency visualization instead of true stacked letter rendering
Requires external WebLogo or seq2logo installation for high-resolution graphical output
Information content uses simplified formula without background frequency or small-sample correction

use_cases.title: Visualize Conserved DNA Binding Motifs
use_cases.description: A bioinformatics researcher identifies conserved transcription factor binding motifs from ChIP-seq peak sequences and generates sequence logos for publication figures.

use_cases.title: Generate Protein Conservation Figures
use_cases.description: A molecular biologist has aligned homologous protein sequences and needs a sequence logo showing conserved and variable positions for a journal article.

use_cases.title: Quick Inspection of Sequence Conservation
use_cases.description: A computational biologist wants to quickly inspect conservation patterns in a small set of aligned promoter sequences before deciding on further analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Demo Sequence Logo
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a demo sequence logo using the motif-logo-generator skill with demo data.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user testing the skill with built-in demo sequences

prompt_templates.title: Generate Logo from FASTA File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the motif-logo-generator skill to read sequences from [input_file] and generate an ASCII logo. Use the --sequences flag to specify the file path.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a FASTA or plain text file with aligned sequences

prompt_templates.title: Export WebLogo Commands for Publication
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the motif-logo-generator skill to generate WebLogo commands for sequences in [input_file]. Save the commands to [output_file] and explain what they do.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs publication-quality PNG output via WebLogo

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis with Custom Output Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the motif-logo-generator skill to analyze sequences from [input_file]. Save the ASCII logo to [output_path]. Summarize which positions show the strongest conservation and which residues dominate each position.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to analyze conservation patterns and save results for documentation

output_examples.input: Demo DNA sequences: ACGTACGT, ACGTACGT, ACGTACGT, ACGTTCGT, ACGAACGT
output_examples.output: ASCII logo showing Position 1 fully conserved as A, Position 2 fully conserved as C, Position 3 fully conserved as G, Position 4 showing mixed T and C, remaining positions fully conserved.

output_examples.input: FASTA file with aligned protein sequences from a conserved domain
output_examples.output: WebLogo command instructions saved to a file, with an explanation of how to run weblogo with the chemistry color scheme at 300 DPI resolution for publication.

best_practices: Ensure input sequences are pre-aligned and have equal length for accurate conservation calculation
Use the --demo flag first to verify the tool works before processing real data files
Save outputs to file using --output to retain results for documentation and sharing
anti_patterns: Using sequences of different lengths without prior alignment, which produces misleading conservation results
Processing raw unaligned sequences as input and expecting meaningful position-by-position conservation
Relying on ASCII logo output as the final figure for publication instead of using WebLogo export

faq.question: What input formats does motif-logo-generator support?
faq.answer: It supports plain text files with one sequence per line and the built-in --demo mode. For FASTA files, the script reads sequences line by line skipping empty lines.

faq.question: Can this tool align sequences for me?
faq.answer: No. The tool requires pre-aligned sequences of equal length. You must align your sequences using a tool like MUSCLE, ClustalW, or MAFFT before using this skill.

faq.question: How does the information content calculation work?
faq.answer: The tool calculates information content as the negative sum of p * log2(p) across residues at each position, where p is the observed frequency. This is a simplified measure of conservation.

faq.question: Can I generate publication-quality PNG images?
faq.answer: Yes, but requires external WebLogo installation. The tool generates WebLogo command instructions that you can run to produce high-resolution PNG files at 300 DPI.

faq.question: Does this tool work for both DNA and protein sequences?
faq.answer: Yes. It accepts any character-based sequences including standard DNA nucleotides (A, C, G, T) and protein amino acids. It calculates frequencies for all residues present.

faq.question: What is the difference between ASCII and WebLogo output?
faq.answer: ASCII output uses block characters (filled squares) to show conservation frequency directly in the terminal. WebLogo output generates shell commands to run the external weblogo tool for a polished, publication-ready image.
